name: jbxapi
documentation:
![Joe Sandbox API v2](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joesecurity/jbxapi/master/img/logo.png)

# API Wrapper

The Joe Sandbox API Wrapper enables you to fully integrate Joe Sandbox into your malware analysis framework. Joe Sandbox is a deep malware analysis platform for analyzing malicious files.

You can use this wrapper with

 * [Joe Sandbox Cloud](https://www.joesecurity.org/joe-sandbox-cloud) — our Cloud hosted instance
 * [On-premise installations of Joe Sandbox](https://www.joesecurity.org/joe-security-products#on-premise) — for even more power and privacy

It is at the same time a powerful implementation of the Joe Sandbox API and also a command line tool for interacting with Joe Sandbox.

# License

The code is written in Python and licensed under MIT.

# Requirements

* Python 2.7 or higher
* Python 3.5 or higher

# Installation

## With Pip

    pip install jbxapi

For upgrading `jbxapi` to a more recent version, use

    pip install --upgrade jbxapi

## Manually

1. Install the python library [`requests`](https://requests.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

        pip install requests

2. Copy `jbxapi.py` to where you need it.

# Documentation

* [Command Line Interface](docs/cli.md)
* [Python API](docs/api.md)

# Credits

* Thanks to [Pedram Amini](https://github.com/pedramamini) for a first wrapper implementation!

# Links

* [Joe Securiy LLC](https://www.joesecurity.org)
* [Joe Security Blog](https://blog.joesecurity.org)
* [Twitter @joe4security](https://twitter.com/joe4security)

modules.name: jbxapi
documentation:
jbxapi.py serves two purposes.

 (1) a light wrapper around the REST API of Joe Sandbox
 (2) a command line script to interact with Joe Sandbox
!/usr/bin/env python3
License: MIT
Copyright Joe Security 2023

functions.name: jbxapi._cli_bytes_from_str
documentation:
Python 2/3 compatibility function to ensure that what is sent on the command line
is converted into bytes. In Python 2 this is a no-op.

functions.name: jbxapi._to_bool
documentation:
Booleans should be submitted as "0" or "1". They can also be missing.

Returns "0", "1" or `None`

functions.name: jbxapi._urllib3_fix_filenames
documentation:
Remove non-ASCII characters from file names due to a limitation of the combination of
urllib3 (via python-requests) and our server
https://github.com/requests/requests/issues/2117
Internal Ticket #3090

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.__init__
documentation:
Create a JoeSandbox object.

Parameters:
  apikey:     the api key
  apiurl:     the api url
  accept_tac: Joe Sandbox Cloud requires accepting the Terms and Conditions.
              https://jbxcloud.joesecurity.org/resources/termsandconditions.pdf
  timeout:    Timeout in seconds for accessing the API. Raises a ConnectionError on timeout.
  verify_ssl: Enable or disable checking SSL certificates.
  retries:    Number of times requests should be retried if they timeout.
  proxies:    Proxy settings, see the requests library for more information:
              https://requests.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user/advanced/#proxies
  user_agent: The user agent. Use this when you write an integration with Joe Sandbox
              so that it is possible to track how often an integration is being used.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.analysis_list
documentation:
Fetch a list of all analyses.

Consider using `analysis_list_paged` instead.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.analysis_list_paged
documentation:
Fetch all analyses. Returns an iterator.

The returned iterator can throw an exception anytime `next()` is called on it.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.submit_sample
documentation:
Submit a sample and returns the submission id.

Parameters:
  sample:       The sample to submit. Needs to be a file-like object or a tuple in
                the shape (filename, file-like object).
  cookbook:     Uploads a cookbook together with the sample. Needs to be a file-like object or a
                tuple in the shape (filename, file-like object)
  params:       Customize the sandbox parameters. They are described in more detail
                in the default submission parameters.

Example:

    import jbxapi

    joe = jbxapi.JoeSandbox(user_agent="My Integration")
    with open("sample.exe", "rb") as f:
        joe.submit_sample(f, params={"systems": ["w7"]})

Example:

    import io, jbxapi

    joe = jbxapi.JoeSandbox(user_agent="My Integration")

    cookbook = io.BytesIO(b"cookbook content")
    with open("sample.exe", "rb") as f:
        joe.submit_sample(f, cookbook=cookbook)

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.submit_sample_url
documentation: Submit a sample at a given URL for analysis.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.submit_url
documentation: Submit a website for analysis.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.submit_command_line
documentation: Submit a commandline for analysis.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.submit_cookbook
documentation: Submit a cookbook.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox._file_size
documentation:
Tries to find the size of the file-like object 'f'.
If the file-pointer is advanced (f.tell()) it subtracts this.

Raises ValueError if it fails to do so.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.submission_list
documentation:
Fetch all submissions. Returns an iterator.

You can give the named parameter `include_shared`.

The returned iterator can throw an exception every time `next()` is called on it.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.submission_info
documentation: Returns information about a submission including all the analysis ids.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.submission_delete
documentation: Delete a submission.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.server_online
documentation: Returns True if the Joe Sandbox servers are running or False if they are in maintenance mode.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.analysis_info
documentation: Show the status and most important attributes of an analysis.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.analysis_delete
documentation: Delete an analysis.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.analysis_download
documentation:
Download a resource for an analysis. E.g. the full report, binaries, screenshots.
The full list of resources can be found in our API documentation.

When `file` is given, the return value is the filename specified by the server,
otherwise it's a tuple of (filename, bytes).

Parameters:
    webid:    the webid of the analysis
    type:     the report type, e.g. 'html', 'bins'
    run:      specify the run. If it is None, let Joe Sandbox pick one
    file:     a writable file-like object (When omitted, the method returns
              the data as a bytes object.)
    password: a password for decrypting a resource (see the
              encrypt-with-password submission option)

Example:

    name, json_report = joe.analysis_download(123456, 'jsonfixed')

Example:

    with open("full_report.html", "wb") as f:
        name = joe.analysis_download(123456, "html", file=f)

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.analysis_search
documentation:
Lists the webids of the analyses that match the given query.

Searches in MD5, SHA1, SHA256, filename, cookbook name, comment, url and report id.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.server_systems
documentation: Retrieve a list of available systems.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.account_info
documentation:
Only available on Joe Sandbox Cloud

Show information about the account.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.server_info
documentation: Query information about the server.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.server_lia_countries
documentation: Show the available localized internet anonymization countries.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.server_languages_and_locales
documentation: Show the available languages and locales

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_machine_info
documentation: Show JoeLab Machine info.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_images_list
documentation: List available images.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_images_capture
documentation: Capture disk image of a machine.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_images_reset
documentation: Reset a disk image of a machine.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_filesystem_upload
documentation:
Upload a file to a Joe Lab machine.

Parameters:
  machine       The machine id.
  file:         The file to upload. Needs to be a file-like object or a tuple in
                the shape (filename, file-like object).

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_filesystem_download
documentation:
Download a file from a Joe Lab machine.

Parameters:
    machine:  The machine id.
    path:     The path of the file on the Joe Lab machine.
    file:     a writable file-like object

Example:

    with open("myfile.zip", "wb") as f:
        joe.joelab_filesystem_download("w7_10", "C:\windows32\myfile.zip", f)

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_network_info
documentation: Show Network info

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_network_update
documentation: Update the network settings.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_pcap_start
documentation: Start PCAP recording.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_pcap_stop
documentation: Stop PCAP recording.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_pcap_download
documentation:
Download the captured PCAP.

Parameters:
    machine:  The machine id.
    file:     a writable file-like object

Example:

    with open("dump.pcap", "wb") as f:
        joe.joelab_pcap_download("w7_10", f)

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox.joelab_list_exitpoints
documentation: List the available internet exit points.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox._decrypt
documentation: Decrypt encrypted files downloaded from a Joe Sandbox server.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox._post
documentation:
Wrapper around requests.post which

    (a) always inserts a timeout
    (b) converts errors to ConnectionError
    (c) re-tries a few times

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox._check_user_parameters
documentation:
Verifies that the parameter dict given by the user only contains
known keys. This ensures that the user detects typos faster.

methods.name: jbxapi.JoeSandbox._raise_or_extract
documentation:
Raises an exception if the response indicates an API error.

Otherwise returns the object at the 'data' key of the API response.
